commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces GitHub skill backups with R2/S3 registry artifact backup and restore, adds package artifact coverage, durable retry and historical seed flows, and a manual Convex backup gate for migrations.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR introduces a new production backup and recovery architecture rather than fixing a current-main bug with an observable failing reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainers need to approve the production recovery cutover and operator runbook after the contributor supplies live bucket proof; this is not a safe autonomous repair task.

target.title: Back up registry artifacts and add migration backup gate
target.body:
## Summary

- Replace the stale GitHub artifact backup path with R2/S3-compatible registry artifact backups for hosted skill versions and plugin/package release artifacts.
- Store a browsable restore catalog under `skills/` and `packages/` with per-version `_meta.json` files plus root `_index.json` files.
- Keep publish-time backup async and non-fatal; failed writes are queued as retry jobs, and the cron drains retries only instead of scanning the full registry.
- Add admin seed/backfill support through `registryArtifactBackups:seedRegistryArtifactBackups` for the initial historical population.
- Add the ClawHub migration lifecycle skill with the manual Convex dashboard `Backup Now` gate before production dry run/apply.

## Backup Shape

- `skills/<owner>/<skill-slug>/_index.json`
- `skills/<owner>/<skill-slug>/<version>/_meta.json`
- `skills/<owner>/<skill-slug>/<version>/<skill files...>`
- `packages/<owner>/<package-name>/_index.json`
- `packages/<owner>/<package-name>/<version>/_meta.json`
- `packages/<owner>/<package-name>/<version>/<artifact.tgz>`

The indexes preserve latest and historical restore lookup metadata without relying on object-store listing or Git history.

## Operator Notes

Required Convex env:

- `REGISTRY_BACKUP_R2_ACCOUNT_ID` or `REGISTRY_BACKUP_S3_ENDPOINT`
- `REGISTRY_BACKUP_BUCKET`
- `REGISTRY_BACKUP_ACCESS_KEY_ID`
- `REGISTRY_BACKUP_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`

Optional:

- `REGISTRY_BACKUP_S3_REGION`
- `REGISTRY_BACKUP_SKILLS_ROOT`
- `REGISTRY_BACKUP_PACKAGES_ROOT`

Recommended smoke after deploy:

- Run a dry-run seed with a tiny batch.
- Run a real seed with `batchSize: 1, maxBatches: 1`.
- Inspect the R2 `_meta.json` and `_index.json` objects for one backed-up skill/package.
- Start the controlled seed/backfill separately.

## Proof

```bash
bun run test convex/registryArtifactBackups.test.ts convex/skills.deleteTags.test.ts convex/skills.ownership.test.ts convex/lib/registryArtifactBackup.test.ts convex/registryArtifactRestore.test.ts convex/functions.test.ts convex/crons.test.ts convex/packages.public.test.ts
# passed: 8 files, 281 tests

bunx tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit --pretty false
# passed

bun run format:check
# passed

bun run lint
# passed

bun run deadcode:ci
# passed

bun run check:peers
# passed

git diff --check
# passed

CONVEX_DEPLOYMENT=dev:small-squirrel-75 VITE_CONVEX_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3212 bunx convex codegen
# passed earlier in this closeout after schema/API changes

CONVEX_DEPLOYMENT=dev:small-squirrel-75 VITE_CONVEX_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3212 bunx convex dev --once --typecheck=disable
# passed: Convex functions ready

codex review --base origin/main
# passed: no discrete/actionable regressions found
```

`bun run ci:static` currently reaches `bun audit` and then fails on transitive `esbuild` advisories reported by Bun (`GHSA-gv7w-rqvm-qjhr`, `GHSA-g7r4-m6w7-qqqr`). The remaining static subcommands above passed.
